name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (١٤١)
```

```english
[The people of] Thamud impugned the apostles (141)
```

```arabic
إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ صَـٰلِحٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (١٤٢)
```

```english
when Salih, their brother, said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]? (142)
```

```arabic
إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ (١٤٣)
```

```english
Indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you. (143)
```

```arabic
فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (١٤٤)
```

```english
So be wary of Allah and obey me. (144)
```

```arabic
وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (١٤٥)
```

```english
I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds. (145)
```

```arabic
أَتُتْرَكُونَ فِى مَا هَـٰهُنَآ ءَامِنِينَ (١٤٦)
```

```english
Will you be left secure in that which is here (146)
```

```arabic
فِى جَنَّـٰتٍ وَعُيُونٍ (١٤٧)
```

```english
—amid gardens and springs, (147)
```

```arabic
وَزُرُوعٍ وَنَخْلٍ طَلْعُهَا هَضِيمٌ (١٤٨)
```

```english
farms and date palms with dainty spathes (148)
```

```arabic
وَتَنْحِتُونَ مِنَ ٱلْجِبَالِ بُيُوتًا فَـٰرِهِينَ (١٤٩)
```

```english
—while you skillfully hew out houses from the mountains? (149)
```

```arabic
فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (١٥٠)
```

```english
So be wary of Allah and obey me, (150)
```

```arabic
وَلَا تُطِيعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَ ٱلْمُسْرِفِينَ (١٥١)
```

```english
and do not obey the dictates of the transgressors, (151)
```

```arabic
ٱلَّذِينَ يُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا يُصْلِحُونَ (١٥٢)
```

```english
who cause corruption in the land and do not bring about reform.’ (152)
```

```arabic
قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسَحَّرِينَ (١٥٣)
```

```english
They said, ‘Indeed you are one of the bewitched. (153)
```

```arabic
مَآ أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُنَا فَأْتِ بِـَٔايَةٍ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (١٥٤)
```

```english
You are just a human being like us. So bring us a sign, should you be truthful.’ (154)
```

```arabic
قَالَ هَـٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةٌ لَّهَا شِرْبٌ وَلَكُمْ شِرْبُ يَوْمٍ مَّعْلُومٍ (١٥٥)
```

```english
He said, ‘This is a she-camel; she shall drink and you shall drink on known days. (155)
```

```arabic
وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابُ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (١٥٦)
```

```english
Do not cause her any harm, for then you shall be seized by the punishment of a terrible day.’ (156)
```

```arabic
فَعَقَرُوهَا فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ نَـٰدِمِينَ (١٥٧)
```

```english
But they hamstrung her, whereupon they became regretful. (157)
```

```arabic
فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (١٥٨)
```

```english
So the punishment seized them. There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith. (158)
```

```arabic
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (١٥٩)
```

```english
Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (159)
```

## (Commentary)

The verses point toward a summary of the story of Salih (عليه السلام) and his people. He is one of the Arab prophets and is mentioned in the Qur’an after Hud (عليه السلام).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ الْمُرْسَلِينَ** (The [people of] Thamud contradicted the apostles) until His saying: **عَلى رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ** (from the Lord of all the worlds). Their meaning has become clear from what has preceded.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَتُتْرَكُونَ فِي ما هاهُنا آمِنِينَ** (Will you be left secure in what is here?) [26:146]. It is apparent that the interrogation is for the purpose of denunciation (الإنكار). The word "what" (ما) is a relative pronoun referring to the blessings (النعم) which He details after His saying: **فِي جَنَّاتٍ وَعُيُونٍ** (in gardens and springs) etc. "Here" (هاهنا) is a demonstrative referring to the present, nearby location, which is the land of Thamud. "Secure" (آمنين) is a circumstantial qualifier (حال) for the pro-agent of "you be left" (تتركون).

The meaning is: You will not be left in these blessings that surround you in this land of yours, while you are given free rein, not being questioned about what you do, secure from any divine reckoning (مؤاخذة إلهية).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فِي جَنَّاتٍ وَعُيُونٍ وَزُرُوعٍ وَنَخْلٍ طَلْعُها هَضِيمٌ** (in gardens and springs, and cornfields and date palms with succulent spathes?) [26:147-148]. This is a detailed exposition (بيان تفصيلي) of His saying: "in what is here" (في ما هاهنا). The date palm was specifically mentioned despite being included in "gardens" due to their great concern for it. The "spathe" (الطلع) in the date palm is like the blossom in other trees, and "succulent" (الهضيم) — according to what has been said — refers to that which is overlapping and tightly packed together.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَتَنْحِتُونَ مِنَ الْجِبالِ بُيُوتاً فارِهِينَ** (and you hew out houses from the mountains skillfully?) [26:149]. Al-Raghib said: "Al-Farah (الفره) — with a *fatha* then a *kasra* as an adjective — means 'insolent' (الأشر). His saying, the Exalted: **وَتَنْحِتُونَ مِنَ الْجِبالِ بُيُوتاً فارِهِينَ** means 'skillfully' (حاذقين), and it is said it means 'insolently' (أشرين)." End of summary. Based on what he chose, the verse would be an exposition of the blessing; based on the other meaning, it would be intended to denounce their insolence and arrogance (بطرهم). In any case, the verse falls within the scope of the interrogation.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ** (So be wary of Allah and obey me) [26:150]. This is a derivation from the preceding denunciation, which carries the sense of a negation.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَلا تُطِيعُوا أَمْرَ الْمُسْرِفِينَ الَّذِينَ يُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلا يُصْلِحُونَ** (and do not obey the bid of the profligate, who cause corruption in the land and do not set things right) [26:151-152]. It is apparent that "bid" or "command" (الأمر) here refers to the opposite of "prohibition" (النهي), evidenced by the prohibition of obeying it. Some have allowed for "command" (الأمر) to mean "affair" or "state" (الشأن), in which case the meaning of obeying their command would be the commoners' imitation (تقليد) and following of them in their actions and their treading of the paths they wish for them to tread.

In any case, the "profligate" (المسرفين) — *those who exceed the bounds of moderation and truth* — refers to the nobles and grandees of the people who are followed, and the address is to the commoners who follow them. As for the masters and nobles, there was no hope for their faith or their following of the truth.

It is possible that the address is to everyone, since the nobles among them also used to imitate their forefathers and obey their command, as they said to Salih (عليه السلام): **أَتَنْهانا أَنْ نَعْبُدَ ما يَعْبُدُ آباؤُنا** (Do you forbid us to worship what our fathers used to worship?) [11:62]. Thus, they were all obeying the command of the profligate, so they were forbidden from it.

He explained the "profligate" (المسرفين) — who are those who transgress against the truth and depart from the limit of moderation — by describing them with His saying: **الَّذِينَ يُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلا يُصْلِحُونَ** (who cause corruption in the land and do not set things right). This is an indication of the actual cause (علة) of the ruling. The meaning is: Be God-conscious (اتقوا الله) and do not obey the command of the profligate because they are corrupters in the land, not reformers, and with corruption (الإفساد), one is not safe from divine punishment, for He is All-Mighty, Possessor of Retribution.

This is because the cosmos (الكون), despite the opposition and conflict (تزاحم) between its parts, is composed with a specific composition through which its parts harmonize with one another in their results and effects. This is like the case of the two pans of a scale; despite their agitation and extreme difference in rising and falling, they are in agreement in determining the weight of the goods being weighed, which is the goal. The human world, which is a part of the cosmos, is likewise. Furthermore, the individual human being, with his various and opposing powers and instruments, is created with a primordial nature (مفطور) to moderate his actions and deeds such that each of his powers receives its predestined share. He has been equipped with an intellect (عقل) that distinguishes between good and evil and gives every possessor of a right his due.

Thus, the cosmos proceeds by the system (النظام) current within it toward righteous, intended goals. Through the complete interconnection between its parts, it delineates for each of its parts a specific path in which it proceeds with specific actions, without deviating from its very center to the right or left, and without swerving through excess (إفراط) or deficiency (تفريط). Indeed, in deviation and swerving there is a corruption (إفساد) of the prescribed system, followed by the corruption of its goal and the goal of the whole. It is necessary that the departure of some parts from the line delineated for them, and the corruption of the order (النظم) imposed for them and for others, entails the contention of the remaining parts against them. If they are able to straighten them and return them to the center of moderation, then so be it; otherwise, they annihilate them and erase their traces to preserve the well-being (صلاح) of the cosmos and maintain its stability (قوام).

Man, who is one of the parts of the cosmos, is not an exception to this universal principle (الكلية). If he proceeds according to what his primordial nature (الفطرة) — *the innate disposition toward truth that Allah placed in every human being* — guides him to, he attains the happiness (السعادة) predestined for him. But if he transgresses the limits of his nature and causes corruption in the land, Allah, the Glorified, seizes him with years of famine, exemplary punishments (المثلات), and various types of deterrents and vengeance, that perhaps he might return to righteousness and correctness. The Exalted said: **ظَهَرَ الْفَسادُ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ بِما كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِي النَّاسِ لِيُذِيقَهُمْ بَعْضَ الَّذِي عَمِلُوا لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ** (Corruption has appeared in land and sea because of what the hands of men have wrought, that He may make them taste something of what they have done, so that they may return) [30:41].

If they persist in corruption despite this, due to its rootedness in their souls, Allah seizes them with the punishment of extirpation (عذاب الاستئصال) and purifies the earth from the filth of their corruption. The Exalted said: **وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ الْقُرى آمَنُوا وَاتَّقَوْا لَفَتَحْنا عَلَيْهِمْ بَرَكاتٍ مِنَ السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلكِنْ كَذَّبُوا فَأَخَذْناهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ** (If the people of the towns had been faithful and Godwary, We would have opened to them blessings from the heaven and the earth. But they denied; so We seized them because of what they used to earn) [7:96]. And He said: **وَما كانَ رَبُّكَ لِيُهْلِكَ الْقُرى بِظُلْمٍ وَأَهْلُها مُصْلِحُونَ** (Your Lord would never destroy the towns unjustly while their people were righteous) [11:117]. And He said: **أَنَّ الْأَرْضَ يَرِثُها عِبادِيَ الصَّالِحُونَ** (My righteous servants shall inherit the earth) [21:105]. This is because if they are righteous, their deeds become righteous, and if their deeds are righteous, they agree with the general system, and through them, the earth becomes fit for their earthly life.

It has become clear from what has passed, firstly: that the reality of the prophetic call (دعوة النبوة) is the reformation (إصلاح) of earthly human life. The Exalted said, narrating from Shu‘ayb: **إِنْ أُرِيدُ إِلَّا الْإِصْلاحَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ** (I do not intend anything but reform so far as I can) [11:88].

Secondly: that His saying: **وَلا تُطِيعُوا أَمْرَ الْمُسْرِفِينَ الَّذِينَ يُفْسِدُونَ** (and do not obey the bid of the profligate, who cause corruption...) etc., despite the simplicity of its expression, relies upon a demonstrative proof (حجة برهانية).

Perhaps in His saying: **وَلا يُصْلِحُونَ** (and do not set things right) after His saying: **الَّذِينَ يُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ** (who cause corruption in the land) is an indication that it was expected of them — since they are human beings possessing a human primordial nature — to act righteously in the land, but they deviated from that nature and exchanged reformation for corruption.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **قالُوا إِنَّما أَنْتَ مِنَ الْمُسَحَّرِينَ** (They said, ‘You are only one of the bewitched) [26:153]. Meaning, one of those who has been bewitched (سحر) time after time until his intellect was overcome. It is also said that the *suhr* (السحر) is the upper part of the stomach, and the *musahhar* (المسحر) is one who has an interior (جوف), so it would be a metaphor for "you are a human being like us who eats and drinks." Thus, his saying after it: **ما أَنْتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُنا** (You are nothing but a human like us) would be a confirmation of it. It is also said that the *musahhar* is one who has a *suhr*, meaning a lung, as if they meant: "you are a breathing human like us."

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ما أَنْتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُنا** (You are nothing but a human like us) until His saying: **عَذابُ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ** (the punishment of a terrible day) [26:154-156]. The word *shirb* (الشرب) with a *kasra* on the *shin* means a share of water. The rest is clear, and the details of the story have already preceded in Surah Hud.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَعَقَرُوها فَأَصْبَحُوا نادِمِينَ** (But they hamstrung her, and then they became regretful) [26:157]. The attribution of the hamstringing (العقر) to the group — even though only one of them hamstrung her — is due to their satisfaction (رضا) with his act. In *Nahj al-Balagha*: "**O people, indeed what brings people together is satisfaction and anger. Indeed, only one man hamstrung the she-camel of Thamud, but Allah encompassed them all with the punishment when they all encompassed it with satisfaction. Thus, the Glorified said: 'But they hamstrung her, and then they became regretful.'**"

His saying: **فأصبحوا نادمين** (and then they became regretful) — perhaps their regret was only upon witnessing the appearance of the signs of the punishment, even though they had said to him after the hamstringing, out of defiance and mockery: **يا صالِحُ ائْتِنا بِما تَعِدُنا إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ** (O Salih, bring us what you threaten us with, if you are one of the apostles) [7:77].

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَأَخَذَهُمُ الْعَذابُ** (So the punishment seized them) until His saying: **الْعَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ** (the All-mighty, the All-merciful) [26:158-159]. The definite article (اللام) is for reference (للعهد), meaning the promised punishment seized them, for Salih had promised them the descent of the punishment after three days, as in Surah Hud. The rest is clear.
